document.title: Risk Management Report for ADD(HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for ADD(HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW) Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, documenting risk analysis, risk control, residual risk evaluation, and production/post-production information.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to document risk management activities for the medical device in accordance with the risk management procedure and referenced standards.
keyPoints: The ADD(HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW) is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector with classification IIa and expected service life of approximately 5 years.
The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
Applicable regulations listed are Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
Risk acceptability criteria define risk levels 1 to 4 as acceptable and 5 to 25 as unacceptable.
Before risk control, 40 risks were identified, with 2 acceptable and 38 unacceptable.
After implementation and verification of risk control measures, all identified residual risks were evaluated as acceptable.

Production and post-production information collection includes customer satisfaction, complaints, inspections, measuring devices, design changes, standards monitoring, market information, process monitoring, and supplier assessment.

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: This is a document for the H&Abyz to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: If the plan changes during the life cycle of the medical device, a record of the changes shall be maintained in the risk management file.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The ADD(HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Contraindications and limitations
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: Refer to A-PMS-004 Post-Market Surveillance report for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

concepts.name: Adverse event reporting
concepts.evidence: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency.

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The model name is ADD(HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW).
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: ADD(HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW)

evidence.claim: The document revision is Revision No. 0 with revision date Nov 7 2023 and description Initial.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date Nov 7 2023 Revision Description Initial
evidence.pageTarget: Risk Management Report for ADD HAD1417MCW HAD1717MCW

evidence.claim: The applicable national and regional regulations listed are EU Regulation 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017; Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The device classification is IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIa

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, replacing film-based systems and supporting real-time diagnosis and treatment planning.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD(HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The device consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and converts read data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.

evidence.claim: The detectors support auto exposure detection technology for use without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: Essential performance includes obtaining acquisition images as intended and avoiding adjacent two-line or blank acquired images.
evidence.evidenceText: Acquisition image is obtained as intended. The acquired image should not have two lines adjacent or be blank.

evidence.claim: Reasonably foreseeable misuse includes maintenance and calibration error of the operator.
evidence.evidenceText: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator.

evidence.claim: The device may be connected to a PC by Ethernet cable or via Wi-Fi.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Supply Box(or Power Adapter & Ethernet cable) can be connected to the PC with an Ethernet Cable. The detector can be connected to a PC via Wi-Fi.

evidence.claim: The product contains software.
evidence.evidenceText: The product contains software.

evidence.claim: The device does not store data.
evidence.evidenceText: This product does not store data.

evidence.claim: The product should be installed by a professional engineer.
evidence.evidenceText: The product should be installed by professional engineer.

evidence.claim: The device is intended to be portable.
evidence.evidenceText: Intended to be designed portable.

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 to 4 are acceptable and risk levels 5 to 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 40 risks were identified and estimated.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 40 risks were identified and estimated.

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 2 risks were acceptable and 38 were unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable region: 2ea Unacceptable region: 38ea

evidence.claim: Risk reduction was required for risks in the Red Zone.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk reduction is required for the risks that belong to the Red Zone.

evidence.claim: Risk control options included inherently safe design and manufacture, protective measures, and information for safety and training.
evidence.evidenceText: inherently safe design and manufacture; protective measures in the medical device itself or in the manufacturing process; information for safety and, where appropriate, training to users

evidence.claim: Verification and effectiveness activities referenced ISO 13485 design and development procedures, usability testing under IEC 62366-1, and safety testing under IEC 60601-1.
evidence.evidenceText: design and development verification according to ISO 13485... Usability test according to IEC 62366-1... Safety Test according to IEC 60601-1

evidence.claim: After risk control measures, all identified residual risks were evaluated in the acceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

evidence.claim: The overall residual risks were determined to be acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: Risk management activities were performed and completed except for production and post-production related activities.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (ADD) risk management activities according to the risk management plan are performed and completed with appropriately designated personnel exclude the production and post-production related activities.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information is to be obtained from A-PMS-004 Post-Market Surveillance report.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to A-PMS-004 Post-Market Surveillance report for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible to collect, review, and manage quality problems in production and post-production phases.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase

evidence.claim: The Quality manager shall report safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency when necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text lists European Union and Republic of Korea regulations and does not mention FDA.
Reviewed by field appears blank in the approval table; only Prepared by and Approved by entries include names and dates.
Text quality contains apparent transcription or OCR issues such as 'corraective', 'vaildation', and inconsistent wording; extracted facts use only text that is directly present.
Risk evaluation after risk control table states 40 acceptable and 0 unacceptable but shows total 38, which conflicts with the surrounding statement that all identified residual risks are acceptable.
Review section cites 'EN 15322-1' and '15322-1', while normative references list EN 15223-1:2021 and ISO 15223-1:2021; this possible standard citation conflict was not corrected.
